title: Filesystem escape + network escape negative tests across all three adapters
goal: Land the canonical escape negative-test suite: a process inside each SandboxAdapter MUST NOT (a) read or write outside its declared binds, (b) reach the network when NetPolicy::DenyAll, (c) escalate privileges to host UID, (d) escape a Job Object / cgroup / Podman namespace, (e) inject input into the host operator's foreground (HBR-QUIET-001). Negative tests are the most-load-bearing assertion that the abstraction's promises are real and not just adapter documentation.
expected_diff_shape: New tests under src/backend/handshake_core/tests/sandbox_escape_negative_tests.rs across all three adapters with per-adapter expected-deny outcomes; new harness module src/backend/handshake_core/src/test_harness/escape_attempts.rs containing the catalog of escape attempts; fixtures under src/backend/handshake_core/tests/fixtures/escape_attempts/.
unblock_conditions: All three adapters + WorkPacketScopeBinder merged (binder provides the in-scope vs out-of-scope partition used by escape tests).
implementation_notes: Escape catalog (each entry maps to a small payload that tries one specific escape): (1) ESC-FS-READ-OUT-OF-BIND: `cat /etc/shadow` or `type C:\Windows\System32\config\SAM` — assert ACCESS_DENIED or non-existence; (2) ESC-FS-WRITE-OUT-OF-BIND: `touch /workspace/forbidden_dir/x` against a binder-produced bind set where forbidden_dir is outside allowed_paths — assert EROFS or ENOENT; (3) ESC-FS-SYMLINK-TRAVERSAL: `ln -s /etc/passwd /workspace/allowed/leak; cat /workspace/allowed/leak` — assert the symlink either does not resolve or resolves to nothing (bind-mounted root is not /etc); (4) ESC-NET-DENY-ALL-DNS: `getent hosts cnn.com` under NetPolicy::DenyAll — non-zero exit; (5) ESC-NET-DENY-ALL-TCP: `bash -c 'exec 3<>/dev/tcp/8.8.8.8/53; echo > &3'` under DenyAll — non-zero exit; (6) ESC-NET-LOOPBACK-ONLY-EXTERNAL: under LoopbackOnly, attempt outbound to 8.8.8.8 — denied; (7) ESC-PRIV-ESCALATE-UID: `id -u` and assert NOT 0 (rootless on Podman/Docker; non-system SID on Windows AppContainer) AND `chown 0:0 /workspace/allowed/x` fails; (8) ESC-NAMESPACE-PID: `ls /proc/1/status` and verify the visible PID 1 is the container init (Name: pause OR podman OR similar) NOT the host operator's process; (9) ESC-DEVICE-ACCESS: `ls /dev` and assert /dev/sda, /dev/nvme0n1, etc are NOT present (only the sandbox-allowed device set); (10) ESC-WIN32-FOREGROUND-INJECT (Windows + WindowsNativeJailAdapter only): attempt SetForegroundWindow via a tiny Win32 test binary inside the AppContainer — assert ERROR_ACCESS_DENIED and assert the focus-audit ledger (cluster A.4 wiring) records NO foreground transition to the operator's pid (HBR-QUIET-001). Each escape attempt is run against each adapter that's available; per-adapter expected outcomes are recorded in the fixture (some adapters provide weaker enforcement on some axes — e.g., Docker without --userns=keep-id may show UID 0 inside, which is a finding to record per RW-1 scoring rather than a test failure for that adapter). Test verdicts: each escape attempt is GREEN if denied, RED if successful, YELLOW if behavior matches the documented weaker-enforcement marker for that adapter. A RED on any adapter blocks the WP integration validation. The harness emits a JSON results blob with per-adapter, per-escape outcomes — written to ../Handshake_Artifacts/sandbox-escape-results-<mt-058-run-id>.json for the Integration Validator.
proof_commands: cargo test -p handshake_core --target-dir ../Handshake_Artifacts/handshake-cargo-target --target x86_64-pc-windows-msvc --features win-native-integration sandbox_escape_negative_tests
risk_if_missed: Without escape negative tests, the trait abstraction's safety promises are advisory only; a regression in adapter wiring (a stray --privileged flag, a missing --read-only, a docker socket bind) ships silently and exposes the host operator's machine.
validator_focus: (a) Every escape attempt is RUN against every available adapter (no silent skip); (b) RED on any adapter blocks WP validation; (c) Win32 foreground-inject test is run on Windows (the HBR-QUIET-001 acid test); (d) results JSON is durable for Integration Validator review.

builder_handoff_correction_20260525.operator_instruction: Operator clarified that completed/pass-like states were not validator-reviewed and instructed Kernel Builder to mark everything ready for validator handoff except the blocked item.
builder_handoff_correction_20260525.current_interpretation: Kernel Builder implementation handoff readiness only; no WP/Integration Validator pass or validator-reviewed completion is claimed.

lifecycle.ready_for_validation_reason: Kernel Builder believes implementation/supporting evidence is ready for Integration Validator handoff; validator review remains pending.

minimum_controls: RED on any escape attempt blocks the WP merge — escape negative tests are load-bearing for the whole cluster B safety story.
Win32 foreground-inject test (HBR-QUIET-001) is gated on Windows but MUST RUN on the Windows CI lane; non-run is a coverage gap not a pass.
Results JSON is captured under ../Handshake_Artifacts/ so Integration Validator has durable evidence (not just transient cargo-test stdout).

integration_validator_review.verdict_reason: SandboxEscapeHarness + 10-entry escape catalog (matches MT-058 contract IDs exactly) + sandbox_escape_negative_tests.rs are present. Always-on tests cover catalog completeness, RED detection via StubAdapter::AlwaysAllow, Skipped accounting for unavailable adapters, OS-restriction skip. persist_to_artifacts writes the durable JSON report. Three material concerns: (1) artifact_root hardcodes 'D:/Projects/.../sandbox-escape-results' violating GLOBAL-PORTABILITY-005; (2) the env-gated wsl2/docker/win-native runs are present but the prior LoopbackOnly silent-DenyAll coercion will likely make ESC-NET-LOOPBACK-ONLY-EXTERNAL spuriously Green (no external reach because there is no network at all); (3) ESC-FS-SYMLINK-TRAVERSAL marks Docker and Wsl2Podman as documented_weaker_enforcement_adapters by default - that turns a true escape (symlink leak) into Yellow not Red, which is honest about runtime behavior but reduces the load-bearing assertion.
hbr_unsatisfied: HBR-QUIET-001: ESC-WIN32-FOREGROUND-INJECT requires a handshake-foreground-inject-probe.exe binary, which is referenced in the fixture spec (escape_attempts.rs:337) but not present in the repo. The Windows lane cannot actually exercise the foreground-inject acid test until that probe binary is built.

integration_validator_review_prior.verdict_reason: Sandbox-escape negative-test catalog product code absent on every git ref. Commit ddd7ed11 ('MT-058 sandbox escape negative tests READY_FOR_VALIDATION', 2026-05-22) touches ZERO product files (verified via git show ddd7ed11 -- src/backend/handshake_core/) despite a commit message describing a 10-entry escape catalog, harness, persist_to_artifacts JSON, and 5/5 always-on tests. Prompt-cited commit hash c3516943 does not exist in this repo. This is the highest-risk-if-absent MT in the batch: it is the load-bearing assertion that the sandbox actually contains escapes.
